assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies this as a webhook compatibility issue stating 'common issue after Kubernetes upgrades due to API version deprecations or webhook compatibility problems'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides both commands: 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration' and 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfiguration'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Offers multiple immediate fixes including patching and deleting the webhook configuration

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Provides explicit delete command: 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfiguration cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Labels the delete option as '(nuclear option)' and positions it as temporary emergency fix, implying validation removal

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes command to check current version and discusses upgrading to compatible version for k8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Advises upgrading cert-manager but specifies v1.14.1 for k8s 1.32 support, not specifically mentioning k8s 1.30 support

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Does not mention failurePolicy settings as an alternative approach

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides verification commands including 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it -- echo "success"'

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Does not discuss failurePolicy configuration options or their associated risks
